measurement_note: Size is n, both grid dimensions. The legal 25/100/200 reverse-capacity tiers span 8x, reach the maximum source dimension, and require n(n-1)/2 adjacent row moves. The remotely Accepted capacity-list greedy is O(n^2), and an independent stable-selection implementation also passes. A correct physical-row simulation that takes fixed eight-fold row snapshots for every adjacent swap performs O(n^3) work, completes every tier, and is rejected only by scaling.

cases.name: benchmark: reverse capacities in a 25 by 25 grid
benchmark: reverse capacities in a 100 by 100 grid
benchmark: reverse capacities in a 200 by 200 grid
